novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 stipulates N, Q, h_A, s_A, and q_A as residualized primitives rather than deriving them from a raw four-cluster outcome table; invariant 3 also falsely says cluster C has an event-time +1 cell in periods 0--3.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The claimed refutation target is generic cluster-deletion diagnostic practice; no quoted published claim says a refitted TWFE leave-one-cluster sign reversal only measures fixed-target influence.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship entry cites goodman_bacon_2021_joe by bibkey only, without a section/equation/page anchor tying the timing-decomposition result to the claimed leave-one-refit gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship; a single finite four-cluster witness plus minimality is not a generic-class obstruction or estimator frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The no-refit corollary claims the displayed witness has x_A^T y_A < N, but Exhibit 9.1 displays only s_A=0.090 > N=0.080 and no separate fixed-residual deleted score.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a raw 4-by-4 outcome table Y, then compute the FWL residualized X and Y, N, Q, h_A, s_A, q_A, and all leave-one coefficients from that table.
Turn the finite witness into an open-class diagnostic: characterize a nonempty region of untreated baselines and common ATT values where refitted TWFE flips sign while BJS/SA remain positive.
Anchor the cluster-diagnostic comparator to an explicit claim in MacKinnon-Nielsen-Webb or downgrade the result from a refutation of practice to a constructive cautionary example.
Add a direct comparison to Goodman-Bacon's decomposition showing why the full-sample timing weights do not determine the leave-one-refit sign after deleting A.

recommended_next_step: Reject this angle unless the drafter can rebuild it from a raw outcome-table witness and lift it from a finite example to a generic-class diagnostic.
